description:
Bug: estimateRemainingPayPeriods() in calculator.go and remainingPayPeriods() in projection.go calculate remaining periods as totalPerYear - count(uploaded stubs). If a paystub upload is missed, the calculator overestimates remaining periods and underestimates needed additional withholding.

Fix: Use pay period dates from the paystubs (already parsed) to determine elapsed periods. For example, with the latest paystub's PayPeriodEnd date and the inferred frequency, calculate how many periods have actually elapsed in the tax year. Alternatively, use YTD gross / per-period gross to infer elapsed count. Both approaches are more robust than counting uploads.

Affected files:
- internal/calc/calculator.go (estimateRemainingPayPeriods, lines 143-174)
- internal/calc/projection.go (remainingPayPeriods, lines 251-260)
- internal/calc/calculator_test.go
- internal/calc/projection_test.go
title: Fix remaining pay periods: use pay period dates instead of upload count

close_reason: Generalized PDF parser: broadened regex patterns, columnar layout support, 11 new test cases, real paystub fixture. Verified working with real paystubs.
description: The PDF parser uses rigid regex patterns that only recognize one paystub format. Need to support common variations: ADP, Paychex, Gusto, Workday, etc. Handle different label names (Total Earnings vs Gross Pay, FWT vs Federal Tax), different date formats, and tabular vs labeled layouts.
title: Generalize PDF parser to handle multiple paystub formats
